title: General Parking on campus for most undergraduates is very difficult to come by because of limited space and high demand. The UCLA transportation department has an application process to obtain a permit. However, barring a disability or a critical need as a commuter (off-campus job, child care, ill family member, etc) odds are very slim to obtain a permit. If accepted, obtaining a carpool parking permit reduces the cost provided that other students sign on to it. There are pay by the hour/day spots available in many of the parking structures; however, they are very pricey. For parking during Summer Sessions, space is less impacted and there is no parking application required. Permits are limited and sold on a first come, first serve basis. Students may visit the Bruin ePermit Portal to purchase a Summer Session permit directly. This map details the locations for pay stations and parking structures around campus.
filename: r_ucla Parking Guide_ Permits, Rates & Daily Passes.pdf

segments.text: General Parking on campus for most undergraduates is very difficult to come by because of limited space and high demand. The UCLA transportation department has an application process to obtain a permit. However, barring a disability or a critical need as a commuter (off-campus job, child care, ill family member, etc) odds are very slim to obtain a permit. If accepted, obtaining a carpool parking permit reduces the cost provided that other students sign on to it. There are pay by the hour/day spots available in many of the parking structures; however, they are very pricey. For parking during Summer Sessions, space is less impacted and there is no parking application required. Permits are limited and sold on a first come, first serve basis. Students may visit the Bruin ePermit Portal to purchase a Summer Session permit directly. This map details the locations for pay stations and parking structures around campus.

Permit Rates • Commuter Student Permit - $258/Quarter • Residence Hall Student Permit - $324/Quarter • Night and Weekend Permit - $150/Quarter • Two-Person Carpool Permit - $216 Total Fee/Quarter • Three-Person Carpool Permit - $132 Total Fee/Quarter

How to Apply During Fall, Winter, and Spring Quarters, students can apply for parking by following the steps below. A new parking application must be submitted for each parking period except Summer Session. Parking applications must be submitted within the application deadline.
1. Log in to the Bruin ePermit Portal Search r/ucla 2. Click on “Add/Edit Student 3. Select the parking structures 4. Submit the application 5. The Transportation office will
